title: 💰 Gold Loan Interest Rates in India: Compare and Apply Online 📈
metaTitle: Gold Loan Interest Rates in India - Compare and Apply Online
metaDescription: Get the best gold loan interest rates in India. Compare and apply online for gold loans from top banks and NBFCs. Check eligibility, documents required, and repayment options.
tableOfContents: How to Apply for a Gold Loan 📝
Gold Loan Interest Rates in India 📊
Comparison of Gold Loan Interest Rates 🤔
Documents Required for a Gold Loan 📁

intro.heading: Introduction to Gold Loans 🤑
intro.content: Are you in need of urgent funds? 🤔 Do you have gold jewelry or coins lying idle at home? 🏠 You can use them to get a gold loan, which is a type of secured loan where you pledge your gold as collateral to get a loan from a bank or non-banking financial company (NBFC). 🏦 In this article, we will discuss gold loan interest rates in India, how to apply for a gold loan, and compare the interest rates offered by different lenders. ✅

sections.heading: How to Apply for a Gold Loan 📝
sections.content: Applying for a gold loan is a straightforward process. You can visit the website of a bank or NBFC, fill out the online application form, and upload the required documents. 📊 You can also visit a branch of the lender and apply in person. 🏢 The lender will assess the value of your gold, check your credit score, and disburse the loan amount based on the loan-to-value (LTV) ratio. 📊 The LTV ratio is the percentage of the gold's value that the lender is willing to lend. For example, if the LTV ratio is 75%, and the value of your gold is ₹1 lakh, the lender will disburse ₹75,000 as the loan amount. 💸

sections.heading: Gold Loan Interest Rates in India 📊
sections.content:
The gold loan interest rates in India vary from lender to lender. 🤔 The interest rates range from 9% to 24% per annum, depending on the lender, loan amount, and tenure. 📈 Some lenders also offer discounts on the interest rates for existing customers or for loans with a higher tenure. 🎁 Here are the gold loan interest rates offered by some of the top lenders in India:
  • SBI: 9.95% - 14.95% per annum 🏦
  • ICICI Bank: 10.5% - 17.5% per annum 🏦
  • HDFC Bank: 11% - 18% per annum 🏦
  • Muthoot Finance: 12% - 24% per annum 🏦
  • Manappuram Finance: 12.5% - 24.5% per annum 🏦

sections.heading: Comparison of Gold Loan Interest Rates 🤔
sections.content:
When comparing gold loan interest rates, you should consider the following factors:
  • Interest rate: The interest rate is the most important factor to consider when comparing gold loan interest rates. 📊
  • Loan tenure: The loan tenure is the period for which you want to take the loan. 📆
  • Loan amount: The loan amount is the amount you want to borrow. 💸
  • Processing fee: The processing fee is the fee charged by the lender for processing the loan application. 📝
  • Prepayment charges: The prepayment charges are the charges levied by the lender if you want to prepay the loan. 💸
You can use a gold loan calculator to calculate the EMI and total interest payable on your gold loan. 📊

sections.heading: Documents Required for a Gold Loan 📁
sections.content:
The documents required for a gold loan vary from lender to lender. 🤔 However, here are some of the common documents required:
  • Identity proof: You need to provide identity proof such as a passport, driving license, or Aadhaar card. 📝
  • Address proof: You need to provide address proof such as a utility bill, rent agreement, or passport. 📝
  • Income proof: You need to provide income proof such as a salary slip, Form 16, or income tax return. 📊
  • Gold ownership proof: You need to provide proof of gold ownership such as a gold bill or a certificate from a jeweler. 💎
You should check with the lender for the specific documents required. 📝

sections.heading: Repayment Options for Gold Loans 📈
sections.content:
The repayment options for gold loans vary from lender to lender. 🤔 However, here are some of the common repayment options:
  • EMI: You can repay the gold loan in equated monthly installments (EMI). 📊
  • Bullet repayment: You can repay the gold loan in a lump sum at the end of the tenure. 💸
  • Overdraft facility: Some lenders offer an overdraft facility, where you can withdraw and repay the loan amount as per your needs. 📈
You should choose a repayment option that suits your financial needs and goals. 📊

faqs.q: What is the interest rate on a gold loan?
faqs.a: The interest rate on a gold loan varies from lender to lender and ranges from 9% to 24% per annum.

faqs.q: How do I apply for a gold loan?
faqs.a: You can apply for a gold loan online or in person by visiting the website or branch of a bank or NBFC.

faqs.q: What are the documents required for a gold loan?
faqs.a: The documents required for a gold loan include identity proof, address proof, income proof, and gold ownership proof.
